steps.title: Recognize the slope from equal outputs
steps.content: Since $f(0) = 7$ and $f(2) = 7$, the linear function outputs the same $y$-value at two different $x$-values. This is only possible if the slope is 0. A linear function with slope 0 is a horizontal line.

steps.title: Write and verify the equation
steps.content: With slope 0 and $f(0) = 7$, the $y$-intercept is 7. The equation is $f(x) = 7$. Verify: $f(0) = 7$ ✓ and $f(2) = 7$ ✓. That matches choice C — C is correct.
